thesis: NON-REBALANCE DAY: Rebalance due = false. Only 1 day since last checkpoint (2026-03-11); 7-day minimum cadence requires holding until at least 2026-03-18. No emergency invalidation conditions have been triggered across any of the 8 holdings — all positions remain above their respective kill-switch thresholds.

PORTFOLIO HEALTH: Weighted average momentum ~33% vs SPY +5.84% — a +27pp alpha spread that justifies holding the current configuration. The three thesis damage flags (LLY near-term softness, SLV marginal MA50 breach, XOM earnings growth decline) are all monitoring flags, not action triggers. The qualitative refresh confirms all four equity anchors have intact fundamental theses with multi-year EPS acceleration (LLY, KLAC, LMT, XOM FY2026-2028 estimates confirmed).

VARIANT VIEW: The do-nothing decision is not passive — it is a deliberate choice to avoid unnecessary turnover when the portfolio's factor exposures (commodity metals + defense + semis + energy) are correctly aligned with the prevailing mixed/defensive regime. Forcing a trade on day 1 of a 7-day cadence would incur slippage costs without a commensurate improvement in expected alpha. The next rebalance window (2026-03-18) will allow re-evaluation of SLV's MA50 status and LLY's momentum trajectory with cleaner data.
risks: MACRO: Continued broad equity distribution (SPY, QQQ, IWM all below MA50) could accelerate into a broader risk-off episode, pressuring even high-momentum names like KLAC (dd63=-22.4%) and TSM (dd63=-12.6%). A USD rally driven by tariff escalation or Fed surprise could compress gold/silver simultaneously, damaging the 33% commodities sleeve in a correlated drawdown.

IDIOSYNCRATIC: SLV marginally below MA50 — if this persists into next weekly close, the kill-switch condition (2 consecutive weekly closes below MA50) would be triggered, requiring immediate evaluation. LLY has now accumulated 4 consecutive weeks of thesis damage flags; if 3m momentum turns negative at next rebalance, the quality anchor argument weakens and a trim/replace decision becomes warranted.

EXECUTION/LIQUIDITY: No execution risks today as no trades are being made. Monitoring checkpoint: SLV weekly close vs MA50 ($77.96); LLY weekly close vs MA50 ($1041.67); KLAC sustained above MA50 ($1445.83) given vol20=45.6%.
falsifiable_checks: SLV kill-switch: if SLV closes below MA50 ($77.96) for 2 consecutive weekly closes (i.e., week ending 2026-03-13 AND 2026-03-20), initiate emergency review and evaluate trim/replace at next available session, regardless of rebalance cadence.

LLY trim trigger: if LLY 3m momentum turns negative (currently +0.39%) at the 2026-03-18 rebalance window, or if close falls below $960 (MA200 + 9% buffer), reduce weight from 8% to 4% and reallocate to GLD or LMT.
trade_of_the_day.why_now: Base case: Do nothing. The 7-day cadence minimum is a hard constraint and no emergency override conditions are present. Portfolio is correctly positioned for the prevailing mixed/defensive regime with a +27pp momentum spread over SPY. Bull case: GLD and SLV continue their commodity metals leadership through March; LMT benefits from ongoing European rearmament spending announcements; KLAC and TSM recover as AI capex cycle reasserts. Bear case: Broad equity distribution accelerates into a 5-10% correction, SLV breaks MA50 decisively, and the mixed regime shifts fully risk-off — requiring emergency rebalancing before the scheduled 2026-03-18 window. Expected edge of trading today: ~0pp (no improvement over holding, turnover costs are pure drag). Holding is the dominant action.
